notes: Built over the call-only dependency DAG: an edge means a caller calls or references a callee, so file dependencies that flow only through types or imports (never a call) are not represented.
File edges are inferred by projecting symbol call edges onto their files; there is no imports/implementation table.
Higher-order calls, dynamic dispatch, and unresolved callbacks are approximated or missed; an edge the adapter could not resolve sets has_unknown_edges on the groups it touches.
Grouping and wave assignment inherit those limits: a missed edge can wrongly split a wave or under-constrain the order.

Cycle groups are mandatory (a file dependency cycle must be ported together), but small-file coalescing is a size heuristic tuned by max_group_loc; independent small files are not force-merged and simply share a wave.
